columns.name: ROW_ID
columns.description: Unique identifier for each order row in the dataset
columns.business_notes: Primary key — typically excluded from analytical visualizations

columns.name: ORDER_ID
columns.description: Unique order identifier combining country code, year, and order number
example_questions: How many unique orders were placed?

columns.name: ORDER_DATE
columns.description: The date when the order was placed by the customer
columns.business_notes: Ranges from 2016-01-04 to 2019-12-30; supports time-series analysis
example_questions: What is the trend of order volume over time?
Which month had the highest sales?

columns.name: SALES
columns.description: Total revenue from the order in USD
columns.business_notes: Can include negative values for returns and refunds; primary revenue metric
example_questions: What is the total sales by region?
What is the average order value?

columns.name: QUANTITY
columns.description: Number of units ordered in this transaction
columns.business_notes: Integer value ranging from 1 to 14; used for volume analysis
example_questions: What is the average quantity per order?
Which products have highest order volume?

columns.name: PROFIT
columns.description: Net profit (or loss) from the order after deducting costs
columns.business_notes: Can be negative (unprofitable orders); critical for profitability analysis
example_questions: What is total profit by product category?
Which customers are most profitable?

columns.name: REGION
columns.description: Geographic region where the order was shipped
columns.business_notes: Four distinct regions: Central, East, South, West; key for geographic analysis
example_questions: Which region has the highest sales?
How does profit vary by region?

columns.name: SEGMENT
columns.description: Customer segment: Consumer, Corporate, or Home Office
columns.business_notes: Only 3 categories; use for slicing and filtering analysis
example_questions: What is the revenue by customer segment?
Which segment is most profitable?

columns.name: CATEGORY
columns.description: Product category: Furniture, Office Supplies, or Technology
example_questions: What is the sales breakdown by category?
Which category has highest profit margin?

columns.name: SUB_CATEGORY
columns.description: Detailed product sub-category under the main category
columns.business_notes: 17 sub-categories total; provides granular product segmentation
example_questions: What is the sales by product subcategory?
Which sub-category is most profitable?

columns.name: CUSTOMER_ID
columns.business_notes: Primary key for customer dimension; format: XX-XXXXX
example_questions: How many unique customers do we have?

columns.name: CUSTOMER_NAME
columns.description: Full name of the customer who placed the order
columns.business_notes: Often used for reporting and customer analysis
example_questions: Who is our top customer by sales?

columns.name: SHIP_MODE
columns.description: Shipping method used for this order
columns.business_notes: Four distinct shipping modes; low cardinality useful for filtering
example_questions: What is the distribution of shipments by mode?
Does shipping mode affect profit?
